decision: Verification is routed by claim risk, freshness, local evidence, privacy, and multidimensional cost. Provider-grounded results are transient verification/display objects. Provider usage is represented as child ActionTrace data inside the cross-action execution ledger.

risk_tiers.name: factual verification
risk_tiers.default: targeted external verification when local support is weak or stale

risk_tiers.name: high consequence
risk_tiers.default: authoritative current sources, explicit scope, stricter cross-check, prominent human review

modes.fast: local context unless current/unsupported risk requires escalation
modes.balanced: bounded targeted web verification for Tier 2/3
modes.strict: stronger source-quality and cross-check rules; uncertainty still allowed
modes.offline: local project and installed packs only
modes.private: local-only unless user approves a minimized cloud request

recorded_facts: Gemini 3 may execute multiple search queries for one prompt and billing can count each search query.
The pricing page checked on this date advertises a 5,000 grounded prompt/request monthly allowance shared across Gemini 3, followed by USD 14 per 1,000 search queries for referenced tiers.

Current terms restrict caching, analysis, training, automated collection, link harvesting, and database/index construction from Grounded Results, Search Suggestions, and associated links, except for narrow listed purposes.
Grounding output and associated links/suggestions are intended for the end user who initiated the prompt and have provider-specific display requirements.
Current terms state additional 30-day storage of prompts, contextual information, and output for grounding-system operation; paid-quota processing references the provider data-processing addendum.
atomik_rules: do not use grounding as a crawler or dataset builder
do not harvest returned links into an index
display grounded results in a provider-compliant distinct component
allow explicit user import of a destination page through source-core
record actual query count when available
recheck terms and pricing before production

privacy_rules: send the claim and minimum excerpt, not the whole vault
remove unrelated personal identifiers where possible
never send secrets or provider keys
use an appropriately configured paid API project by default for production private-vault workflows
do not claim zero retention when provider terms state operational retention
cost_controls: reuse durable imported sources rather than provider-grounded output
record all provider calls as ActionTrace children
distinguish estimated, provider-reported, and billed costs
relate cost to accepted verification outcomes
stop_conditions: local-only policy with insufficient local evidence

rules: require explicit policy for external fallback
emit parent and child ActionTrace records
do not capture raw content by default
